intro: Thrincia tuberosa, also known as Devil's Claw or Graskop, is a perennial plant in the family Pedaliaceae. The roots of Thrincia tuberosa have been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, pain-relieving, and antipyretic properties, providing relief from arthritis, fever, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Thrincia tuberosa include harpagoside, phytosterols, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Thrincia tuberosa include capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to alleviate joint pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
